text: Always apply sunscreen as the last step of your skincare routine, even on cloudy days or when staying indoors.
Double cleansing — first with an oil-based cleanser, then a water-based one — removes makeup and sunscreen more effectively.
Apply your skincare products from thinnest to thickest consistency for best absorption.
Retinol is most effective when applied at night. Always pair it with SPF in the morning.
Drinking enough water helps maintain skin elasticity and gives you a natural glow.
Use a silk pillowcase to reduce overnight friction on your skin and hair.
Pat, don't rub — always pat your skin dry after washing to prevent irritation.
Vitamin C serum in the morning boosts radiance and reinforces your sunscreen's protection.
Use a lip balm with SPF during the day — lips are often forgotten in sun protection routines.
A light under-eye cream applied with your ring finger prevents pulling the delicate skin.
Wash your makeup brushes every 1–2 weeks to prevent bacteria buildup and breakouts.
Primer creates a smooth base for makeup and significantly extends its wear time.
Curl your lashes before mascara — it opens up the eyes and makes them appear larger.
Setting spray is the secret to long-lasting makeup, especially in humid weather.
A bold lip instantly elevates any simple outfit — keep the rest of your makeup minimal to balance it.
Use a peach or salmon-toned concealer to neutralise under-eye darkness before applying your regular concealer.
Highlight the inner corners of your eyes to make them appear brighter and more awake.
Apply blush to the apples of your cheeks and blend upward for a lifted, fresh look.
Knowing your undertone — warm, cool, or neutral — makes finding the right foundation shade much easier.
Colours opposite on the colour wheel cancel each other out — perfect for colour-correcting redness or dark spots.
Exfoliate 2–3 times a week to remove dead skin cells and keep your complexion radiant.
Hyaluronic acid works best applied on damp skin — it pulls moisture from the air into your skin.
Sleeping with your hair tied back prevents oils and product buildup from transferring to your skin overnight.
Facial massage improves circulation and gives skin a natural, healthy glow over time.
Niacinamide is one of the most versatile ingredients — it brightens, minimises pores, and controls oil.
